ddls:
CREATE TABLE "hosts" (
  "host_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each host */,
  "host_name" TEXT /* Name of the host (e.g., spider-199-21-99-105.yandex.com) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the host */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the host */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region of the host */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the host */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("host_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "host_id" INTEGER /* ID of the host making the request */,
  "reqs" INTEGER /* Number of requests */,
  "kbytes" REAL /* Total bytes transferred */,
  "percent_bytes" REAL /* Percentage of total bytes transferred */,
  "percent_reqs" REAL /* Percentage of total requests */,
  "request_method" TEXT /* HTTP method used (e.g., GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) */,
  "request_protocol" TEXT /* HTTP protocol used (e.g., HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2) */,
  "request_status_code" INTEGER /* HTTP status code returned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_requests_host_id FOREIGN KEY ("host_id") REFERENCES hosts ("host_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_types" (
  "request_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request type */,
  "request_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the request type */,
  "request_type_description" TEXT /* Description of the request type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_details" (
  "request_detail_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request detail */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request */,
  "request_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the request type */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date the request was made */,
  "request_time" TEXT /* Time the request was made */,
  "request_header" TEXT /* HTTP headers of the request */,
  "request_body" TEXT /* HTTP body of the request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_detail_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_details_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES requests ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_request_details_request_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_type_id") REFERENCES request_types ("request_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, analyst, developer) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date the platform was accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time the platform was accessed */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method used to access the platform (e.g., login, API key) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "server_type" TEXT /* Type of server (e.g., web server, database server) */,
  "operating_system" TEXT /* Operating system running on the server */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "server_performance" (
  "server_performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server performance metric */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., CPU usage, memory usage, disk usage) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date the metric was recorded */,
  "metric_time" TEXT /* Time the metric was recorded */,
  "metric_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the metric (e.g., percentage, bytes, seconds) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_server_performance_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_settings" (
  "alert_setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert setting */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., CPU usage, memory usage, disk usage) */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Threshold value for the metric */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Level of the alert (e.g., warning, critical) */,
  "notification_method" TEXT /* Method used to notify the alert (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_settings_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "alert_setting_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert setting */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Time the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_alert_setting_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_setting_id") REFERENCES alert_settings ("alert_setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_validation" (
  "data_validation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data validation rule */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., CPU usage, memory usage, disk usage) */,
  "validation_rule" TEXT /* Validation rule for the metric */,
  "validation_level" TEXT /* Level of the validation (e.g., warning, critical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_validation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_validation_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_encryption" (
  "data_encryption_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data encryption setting */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., CPU usage, memory usage, disk usage) */,
  "encryption_method" TEXT /* Encryption method for the metric */,
  "encryption_key" TEXT /* Encryption key for the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_encryption_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_encryption_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "backup_settings" (
  "backup_setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each backup setting */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., CPU usage, memory usage, disk usage) */,
  "backup_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of backups (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "backup_retention" INTEGER /* Number of backups to retain */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("backup_setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_backup_settings_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "backups" (
  "backup_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each backup */,
  "backup_setting_id" INTEGER /* ID of the backup setting */,
  "backup_date" TEXT /* Date the backup was taken */,
  "backup_time" TEXT /* Time the backup was taken */,
  "backup_file" TEXT /* File containing the backup data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("backup_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_backups_backup_setting_id FOREIGN KEY ("backup_setting_id") REFERENCES backup_settings ("backup_setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "audit_log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., CPU usage, memory usage, disk usage) */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date the change was made */,
  "change_time" TEXT /* Time the change was made */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "permission_description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id", "permission_id")
)
